input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest .NET SDK versions
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install (e.g., .NET 9)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find exact name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK & Dependencies

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components automatically, but to manage them explicitly:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed
$androidSdkPath = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Or check:
dotnet android sdk info

# Discover available Android API levels
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept licenses
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 4. Install Java JDK (required for Android builds)

```powershell
# MAUI bundles Microsoft OpenJDK, but if needed separately:
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install JDK 17 (required for current Android tooling)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --silent

# Verify
java --version
```

## 5. Enable Windows App SDK (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Discover available versions
winget search Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK

# Usually installed with Visual Studio, but standalone:
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppRuntime.1.6 --silent
```

## 6. Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows deployment)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1"
```

## 7. (Optional) Install Visual Studio with MAUI components

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --silent `
    --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat --passive"
```

## 8. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# List MAUI templates to confirm setup
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (ensure emulator is running)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 9. (Optional) Set Up Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "system-images"

# Install an image (pick appropriate API level)
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create AVD
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_API35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel"

# Launch emulator
& "$androidSdkPath\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_API35
```

---

**Key tips:**
- Replace `net9.0` with whatever `dotnet --list-sdks` reports as your major version.
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to keep Android SDK components in sync.
- If builds fail with SDK version errors, `dotnet workload repair` often fixes them.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for hardware-accelerated Android emulation (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
